itinerary_breakdown.description: At the airport you will be greeted by our Jetwing Representative and your chauffeur guide who will take you to the beach resort town of Negombo. Rest and recoup from any jet lag. Negombo is a buzzing hub of contrasts and cultures. If you feel like exploring, your chauffeur guide will show you around some of the most interesting sights and landmarks and perhaps take a boat ride along the Dutch canal. Or, you can relax on the most famous golden sands of Negombo beach.

Leave Negombo’s coastline and head inland. Drop by the Elephant Care Relief Foundation - a charity that looks after domesticated elephants. Meet an elephant expert and enjoy the unique opportunity to walk with and bathe elephants. After lunch, our sightseeing tour of Sri Lanka continues to the town of Sigiriya - a key cornerstone in the cultural triangle. In the evening, climb the amazing Sigiriya rock fortress - a World Heritage Site. This 5th Century marvel in the sky is an architectural masterpiece carved from a massive rock.

Learn about the better kept ruins of ancient Ceylon at the UNESCO World Heritage City of Polonnaruwa - the medieval capital of ancient Lanka from the 11th to 13th century A.D. Take a walking tour to the ruins of Lankathilaka, Thuparamaya, Royal Palace, Rankoth Vehera and Gal Viharaya. On your return, take an exhilarating jeep ride with an expert naturalist guide to the Minneriya or Kaudulla National Park - a sanctuary is a home to wild elephants, wild buffaloes, sambar deer, crocodiles, sloth bear and more.

After breakfast, set off to the historic hill capital of Kandy. En route, visit a spice garden at Matale to see how Sri Lankan spices are grown and processed. Arrive in Kandy - the eminent centre of Buddhism and the last stronghold of the Sinhalese kingdom. After lunch, take a drive away from town to see an ancient temple complex which comprises three shrines: Embekke, Lankathilaka and Gadaladeniya featuring intricate carvings thousands of years old. Enjoy a scenic drive back and spend your evening at leisure.

A day of discovering Kandy awaits you. Explore the town at your leisure. Pay homage to the Temple of the Tooth Relic which houses Sri Lanka’s most sacred relic - a tooth of the Lord Buddha. Drive to the Peradeniya Botanical Gardens, visit the Udawattekele forest reserve - situated right in the heart of the town, or the bustling streets and market places. Take in the views from upper Lake drive. In the evening, attend a performance featuring traditional dances from the different regions of Sri Lanka.

Take the upcountry train into the mountains and enjoy the breathtaking views along the way. From Nanu Oya train station ride to Nuwara Eliya - the land of Ceylon tea. After a rest, drive around the old colonial town of Nuwara Eliya - often referred to as “Little England” with its colonial bungalows, Tudor-style hotels, well-tended hedgerows and pretty gardens shrouded in cool misty air. Take a guided stroll past the renowned golf course, the colonial members only Hill Club and English styled cottages and Victoria Park.

After breakfast, take a very scenic drive down the mountains to Colombo, through rolling tea plantations, lush paddy fields and into the urban sprawl. After a rest from your journey, explore Colombo with your chauffeur guide: visit the Pettah market, the colonial side of Fort, the Hindu Temple at Sea Street, the Dutch Church at Wolfendal or head for the shopping malls for some bargains. In the evening, enjoy the vibrant nightlife of Colombo, or relax and get ready to explore another island paradise tomorrow.

After breakfast, say goodbye to Sri Lanka and drive to catch your flight to Male. From the Male airport you will be paged by us and transferred to your resort via speed boat or by sea plane. Check in and spend the afternoon at leisure. Relax and enjoy spectacular sunsets - Maldivian style, and if you wish you may opt for a night-fishing experience by boat. Catch your fish or learn how to do so and it will be prepared for your dinner if you wish.

After an early breakfast at your resort, take a boat ride to watch pods of dolphins up close as they play in the ocean. The Indian Ocean is home to a treasure trove of colourful tropical fish and sea creatures. The Maldives is also one of the best locations for scuba diving and snorkelling, so take the opportunity to enjoy some Manta Ray watching. Return at sunset to the cosy comforts of your luxurious beach resort for a scrumptious dinner and a restful night’s sleep.

After breakfast, get ready for another day of adventure in an undersea paradise. Go scuba diving or snorkelling in the pristine waters regarded as one of the best places in the world to do so. There are over 700 species of multi-coloured tropical fish and other marine life for you to witness in their natural habitat. If the turtles are willing, you may get the amazing opportunity to swim with them, or above them - a truly incredible experience. Back ashore, sunsets, dinner and rest awaits.
As all good things must come to an end, we bid you farewell as our chauffeur guide takes you to the Airport to meet your departure flight home.

ocean_and_wildlife_adjacencies: Dive into unique activities, like a sunrise hot air balloon ride over the Sigiriya rock fortress or snorkeling in the crystal-clear waters of the Maldives. Create memories that linger long after the journey ends.

itinerary_breakdown.description: At the airport you will be greeted by your chauffeur guide who will take you to your first destination high in the central mountains of Sri Lanka. Your journey will give you a quick snapshot of the incredible diversity of our island as you travel from the coast, follow the paddyfields in the low valleys and climb into the high mountains covered in tea or lush forest. The steep, uphill drive to Elkaduwa through winding roads often bathed in mist, will be an unforgettable one.

Breakfast at leisure, then explore the hill capital of Kandy, eminent as the centre of Buddhism and the stronghold of the last Sinhalese kingdom. Explore the town at your leisure. Pay homage to the Temple of the Tooth Relic, which houses Sri Lanka’s most sacred relic - a tooth of the Lord Buddha. Visit the Udawattekele forest reserve or the bustling streets and market places. Take in the views from upper Lake drive. Back at Ashburnham, follow the waterfall trail for a quick dip - with views.

Start the day with a tea experience tour. Join Ashburnham’s own field manager on his rounds of the estate comprising 100 acres of tea. Watch the tea pluckers expertly pick their ‘two leaves and a bud’. Learn more about tea cultivation and head back with the pickers to weigh the crop. Set off to explore the foothills of the knuckles range - a unique cloud forest, and a World Heritage Site. You can take the scenic route and visit a few waterfalls along the way too.

Take a ride that is considered to be one of the most epic train journeys in the world. The pace is leisurely, but the wonderful scenery you encounter along the way makes you forget the time and everything else. The line winds through villages, forests and tea estates. It curves round high mountain passes giving you magnificent views of waterfalls and valleys beyond. This is an unforgettable way to catch a glimpse of real Sri Lanka. After the journey, rest or visit Ravana Ella waterfall.

In this 5-hour trek you will experience the true beauty of Sri Lanka’s mountain region, while taking in all the famous landmark attractions Ella is known for. Walk to the incredible Nine Arch bridge - perfect for those social media photos. Visit the Demodara railway station, renowned for its ‘loop’ - a unique feature for turning trains around, and the only one of its kind in Sri Lanka. Then continue to Little Adam’s Peak, a tough trek, but worth it for the amazing views especially at sunset.

Enjoy the scenic drive from Ella to Wellawaya. Your stay is a unique, agro-luxury resort. Nestled in a low valley amidst lush paddy fields circled by mountains, Jetwing Kaduruketha is designed to resemble a traditional village surrounded by nature. The fields around the property are harvested by the villagers. You can learn all about rice cultivation and the local traditions and ceremonies intrinsically linked to it, perhaps take part in some yourself. Later, visit a viewpoint to catch a glimpse of the magnificent Diyaluma waterfall.

In the morning, explore the nearby villages. Following the overgrown footpaths through fields and river banks you will see and experience the true essence of rural life in Sri Lanka. Along the way, greet the locals, taste some homemade treats at a tiny wayside hut, drink a coconut or some herbal tea. Stop to visit the Kirindi Oya river and enjoy a refreshing dip before continuing your leisurely walk through the paddy fields. At nightfall, explore the jungle trails nearby to spot some nocturnal wildlife.

After a leisurely breakfast, set off to explore along the nearby forest trails to Ella Wala waterfall hidden deep inside the jungle. At just 4-5 metres tall, but beautiful with a natural pool perfect for bathing and enjoying a picnic lunch. In the afternoon, trek to visit the spectacular Diyaluma Falls - the 2nd highest waterfall in Sri Lanka. Cascading at different levels with natural pools on the top, the main water drop is 220-metres high. The trek is very challenging, but not to be missed.

A scenic drive to the south coast, and Galle - Sri Lanka’s oldest living city dotted with historic sites with a fusion of European and Arabic cultural and architectural influences. In the afternoon, explore the Galle Fort with its cobbled streets and learn the history behind its Ramparts. A hive of activity, arts and culture it is one of the few World Heritage listed sites in which a bustling population continues to thrive with alleys lined with restaurants, art galleries and shops selling jewellery and antiques.
As all good things must come to an end, we bid you farewell as our chauffeur guide takes you to the Airport to meet your departure flight home

ocean_and_wildlife_adjacencies: Traverse through nature’s wonders as you witness breathtaking wildlife spectacles. Whether it’s a safari adventure or a marine escapade, each moment is a chance to connect with the untamed beauty of the world.

tour_overview.package_name: Wrecks & Reefs | Snorkeling, Diving & Marine life Tour in Sri Lanka

itinerary_breakdown.description: At the airport you will be greeted by your chauffeur guide who will take you to the fishing village of Kalpitiya in the north western coast. The Kalpitiya beach is a long, narrow stretch of sand, also accessible by boat from across the Kalpitiya lagoon. Dolphins, whales and kite surfing are the main attractions that draw visitors to this relatively undeveloped peninsula. Hundreds of dolphins can often be seen playing in the offshore waters, you can take a boat ride to catch some close-up views.

After breakfast, head down to snorkel or dive in the renowned Bar Reef. A must for keen scuba divers and snorkelers, this is the largest coral reef in Sri Lanka and only an hour’s boat ride from Kalpitiya. Beautiful and unspoilt, Bar Reef spans around 307 Sq.km and is home to 156 coral species and 283 species of fish, with frequent sightings of manta rays, reef sharks and turtles. Despite its natural beauty, the western peninsular area of Kalpitiya is remarkably untouched by tourism.

A scenic drive to the south coast and Galle - Sri Lanka’s oldest living city dotted with historic sites with a fusion of European and Arabic cultural and architectural influences. In the afternoon, explore the Galle Fort with its cobbled streets and learn the history behind its Ramparts. A hive of activity, arts and culture it is one of the few World Heritage listed sites in which a bustling population continues to thrive with alleys lined with restaurants, art galleries and shops selling jewellery and antiques.

With its impressive coral reefs and abundance of tropical fish, Hikkaduwa is considered as one of the best places for snorkelling and diving on the Southwest coast. Snorkel or dive in the Conch Reef: 20 metres down and historically significant, being one of the first oil tankers of the Shell Company which sank in 1903 as she struck the Akarta rock. Probably one of the best and easily accessible wrecks in Sri Lanka, with many nooks and crannies within the wreck filled with marine life.

Snorkel or dive in Unawatuna. This 3-hour scuba diving session gives you a wonderful glimpse of Sri Lanka’s colourful corals, mystery wrecks and over 170 species of reef fish, turtles, octopuses and shellfish; perhaps even Sri Lanka’s giant - the whale shark. Travel to snorkel or dive in Weligama - an interesting blend of a lively fishing village and beach resort. The sandy stretch is attractive away from the main section with coves west of the centre and the ocean is perfect for snorkelling and diving too.
As all good things must come to an end, we bid you farewell as our chauffeur guide takes you to the Airport to meet your departure flight home.

ocean_and_wildlife_adjacencies: Indulge in exclusive stays at Jetwing’s handpicked coastal properties, offering a perfect blend of modern comforts and serene oceanfront views, ensuring a relaxing and rejuvenating experience after each day’s adventure.
Dive into the rich maritime history of Sri Lanka as you explore captivating shipwrecks and vibrant coral reefs, creating unforgettable memories in the depths of the Indian Ocean.
Immerse yourself in a world of colorful marine life, from elusive sea turtles to dazzling schools of fish. Discover the joy of snorkeling and diving in crystal-clear waters.
Our experienced guides and certified dive instructors ensure not just a thrilling expedition but a safe one too. Dive with confidence as you explore the mesmerizing wrecks and reefs of Ceylon.

tour_overview.package_name: Courtyards & Verandahs – Geoffrey Bawa Architecture Tour in Sri Lanka

Rest and recoup from any jet lag. Negombo is a buzzing hub of contrasts and cultures. If you feel like exploring, your chauffeur guide will show you around some of the most interesting sights and landmarks and perhaps take a boat ride along the Dutch canal. Or, you can relax on the most famous golden sands of Negombo beach.

Pay an early morning visit to a ‘lellama’ - Negombo’s bustling fish market. Watch the fishermen bring in their night’s catch for sale. Leave the coast and head inland. Break your journey at Anuradhapura - the first capital of ancient Ceylon, reigning for over a thousand years. Take a walking tour with your chauffeur guide visiting the Sacred Sri Maha Bodhi, Ruwanweli seya and Mihintale. Later, arrive at Bawa’s iconic landmark, Heritance Kandalama. A tranquil haven built into the rockface and surrounded by jungle, blending in perfectly.

itinerary_breakdown.route: Kandalama to Sigiriya to Polonnaruwa to Kandalama
itinerary_breakdown.description: Early morning, climb the amazing Sigiriya rock fortress - a World Heritage Site. This 5th Century marvel is an architectural masterpiece carved from a massive rock. In the afternoon, learn about the better kept ruins of ancient Ceylon at the UNESCO World Heritage City of Polonnaruwa - the medieval capital of ancient Lanka from the 11th to 13th century A.D. Take a walking tour of the ruins at Lankathilaka, Thuparamaya, Royal Palace, Rankoth Vehera and Gal Viharaya and Vatadage and be awed by their lasting grandeur.

En route to Kandy, visit the Matale Heritage Centre to see the designs of Mrs. Ena de Silva - a close friend and collaborator of Geoffrey Bawa who contributed many pieces to his projects. Arrive in Kandy - eminent centre of Buddhism and the last stronghold of the Sinhalese kingdom. Pay homage to the Temple of the Tooth Relic, which houses Sri Lanka’s most sacred relic - a tooth of the Lord Buddha. Visit the bustling streets and market places. Take in the views from upper Lake drive.

Enjoy a scenic drive to the hills of Bandarawela through lush tea plantations. Drop by the Chapel of the Good Shepherd’s Convent - designed by Geoffrey Bawa and Ulrik Plesner. Continue to Ella - a small but popular town nestled high in the mountains and famed for its breathtaking views. Stop to admire the cascading Ravana Ella waterfall and the iconic Demodara Nine Arch Bridge. Continue to your stay for the night - the 98 Acres Resort & Spa; an elegant, chic hotel set amidst a sprawling tea estate.

On the way to Galle, visit two lesser known Bawa creations: the Ruhunu University Campus and the Matara Post Office. Stop for lunch at Heritance Ahungalla - another Bawa inspired design. Continue to Galle - Sri Lanka’s oldest living city dotted with historic sites. A hive of activity, arts and culture it’s one of the few World Heritage sites where a bustling population continues to thrive with alleys lined with restaurants, art galleries and shops selling jewellery and antiques. Overnight stay at Jetwing Lighthouse - Bawa’s architectural masterpiece.

On your way to Colombo, take a break for an alfresco lunch at the Lunuganga Estate - Bawa’s country home. Explore Colombo with your chauffeur guide: visit the Pettah market, the colonial side of Fort, the Hindu Temple at Sea Street, the Dutch Church at Wolfendal or head for the shopping malls for some bargains. Visit Bawa’s residence at No. 11 for a tour and continue the evening with dinner at his former office - the Gallery Cafe, perhaps in the company of Channa Daswatte, Bawa’s protege.
As all good things must come to an end, we bid you farewell as our chauffeur guide takes you to the Airport to meet your departure flight home.

At the airport you will be greeted by our Jetwing Representative and your chauffeur guide who will take you to the beach resort town of Negombo. Rest and recoup from any jet lag. Negombo is a buzzing hub of contrasts and cultures. If you feel like exploring, your chauffeur guide will show you around some of the most interesting sights and landmarks and perhaps take a boat ride along the Dutch canal. Or, you can relax on the most famous golden sands of Negombo beach.

After breakfast, visit the Muneeshwaram Temple at Chilaw. Over a thousand years old and an important temple complex for Hindus in Sri Lanka, Munneswaram is one of five ancient coastal kovils called the Pancha Ishwarams dedicated to Shiva along the island’s shoreline. The temple has seen much destruction and reconstruction over the years, but has survived the centuries well in spite of it. The Munneswaram Festival is a popular four-week-long celebration and so is the Navarathri festival. Afterwards proceed to Kandy and rest at leisure.

After breakfast, proceed to Nuwara Eliya visiting Sri Baktha Hanuman Temple, Ramboda en route. Situated on a scenic mountain summit, the temple houses an 18 feet Hanuman statue - the tallest in Sri Lanka. Travel on to Nuwara Eliya stopping at Seetha Amman Temple in Seetha Eliya on the way. An important site on the Ramayana trail, Sita Amman is the only Hindu temple dedicated to Princess Sita in Sri Lanka. It is also close to the historical Ashoka Vatika, now known as Hakgala Botanical Gardens.

After breakfast travel to Ella - a popular town nestled high in the mountains and famed for its breathtaking views. The Ravana Falls is a magnificent cascade around 25m tall and considered to be the widest waterfall in Sri Lanka. As per legend, King Ravana hid Sita in the caves behind the falls and deep within the Ravana Ella Wildlife Sanctuary, the cave entrance is around 5km away from the Falls. Later in the afternoon enjoy a city tour in Ella and spend a leisurely evening.

After breakfast, proceed to Kataragama - a renowned pilgrimage town sacred to Buddhist, Hindu and indigenous Vedda people, with devotees flocking from near and far. The site is included in the Ramayana Trail as the God of Kataragama is believed to have arrived from India. After a scenic descent from Ella, visit the Kataragama shrine, dedicated to God Skanda (Murugan) the God of Love and War. There are also shrines dedicated to Gods and Goddesses such as Murugan temple, Gana Devale, Vishnu Devale and Suniyam Devale.

After breakfast, head to the historic city of Galle. Its Portuguese and Dutch heritage is well present to this day. Visit the Galle Fort - one of the best preserved fortifications in Asia, the Maritime Museum, the Galle National Museum and the famous lighthouse. In the afternoon visit the Rumassala Rock - said to have originated from parts of Mount Dronagiri, dropped by Hanuman while carrying medicinal plants. Sita too, is said to have stayed here during her time in Lanka. Evening at leisure by the sea.

After breakfast proceed to Colombo. Stop en route at Seenigama temple set on a tiny islet. This shrine is dedicated to the local god who is said to protect fishermen and their boats. It is a significant location in the Ramayana trail. Proceed to Colombo and visit the Panchamuga Anjaneyar Temple - dedicated to Anjaneyar in Sri Lanka and the only temple in the world to own a chariot of Anjaneyar. Enjoy the rest of the day sightseeing in Colombo and indulge in some shopping too.
As all good things must come to an end, we bid you farewell as our chauffeur guide takes you to the Airport to meet your departure flight home.

If you have spare time, your chauffeur guide will introduce you to some of Colombo’s curious sites such as the vibrant markets of Pettah, old city of Fort, the Hindu Temple at Sea Street and the Dutch Church at Wolfendal or alternatively to indulge in some shopping.

From the hills of Nuwara Eliya descend to the jungles of Yala. The Yala National Park is famous for the Sri Lankan Leopard (a unique sub species). Yala is also considered to have the highest densities of leopards in the world. Sri Lanka’s ‘Big Five’ can be spotted here too - elephants, sloth bears, sambur and crocodiles, as well as leopards. Take an evening jeep ride with our naturalist guide and keep your fingers crossed - you may well spot them all and other wild life too.
